const
  option_descriptor_t * find_option_descriptor (const options_config_t * config,
  const char * opt_name)


Find option descriptor by short or long name.


Definition at line 1487 of file builder.c.


1487                                                                                                         {
1488   if (!opt_name || opt_name[0] == '\0')
1489     return NULL;
1490 
1491   // Handle short options: -x (single dash, single char)
1492   if (opt_name[0] == '-' && opt_name[1] != '-' && opt_name[1] != '\0') {
1493     // This is a short option like -x
1494     char short_char = opt_name[1];
1495     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1496       if (config->descriptors[i].short_name == short_char) {
1497         return &config->descriptors[i];
1498       }
1499     }
1500     // Short option not found
1501     return NULL;
1502   }
1503 
1504   // Handle long options: --name (double dash)
1505   if (opt_name[0] == '-' && opt_name[1] == '-' && opt_name[2] != '\0') {
1506     const char *long_name = opt_name + 2; // Skip the '--'
1507     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1508       if (strcmp(config->descriptors[i].long_name, long_name) == 0) {
1509         return &config->descriptors[i];
1510       }
1511     }
1512     // Long option not found
1513     return NULL;
1514   }
1515 
1516   // Not a recognized option format
1517   return NULL;
1518 }










int
  format_option_default_value_str (const option_descriptor_t * desc, char * buf,
  size_t bufsize)


Format option default value as a string.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


buf Output buffer


bufsize Size of buffer


Returns


Number of characters written to buf


Definition at line 77 of file builder.c.


77                                                                                                 {
78   if (!desc || !buf || bufsize == 0) {
79     return 0;
80   }
81 
82   // For callback options with enums, look up the enum string by matching the default value
83   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->metadata.enum_values && desc->default_value) {
84     int default_int_val = 0;
85     memcpy(&default_int_val, desc->default_value, sizeof(int));
86 
87     // Try to find matching enum value
88     if (desc->metadata.enum_integer_values) {
89       for (size_t i = 0; desc->metadata.enum_values[i] != NULL; i++) {
90         if (desc->metadata.enum_integer_values[i] == default_int_val) {
91           return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", desc->metadata.enum_values[i]);
92         }
93       }
94     } else {
95       // Fallback: assume sequential 0-based indices if integer values not provided
96       // Count enum values to check bounds
97       size_t enum_count = 0;
98       while (desc->metadata.enum_values[enum_count] != NULL) {
99         enum_count++;
100       }
101       if (default_int_val >= 0 && (size_t)default_int_val < enum_count) {
102         return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", desc->metadata.enum_values[default_int_val]);
103       }
104     }
105   }
106 
107   // For callback options storing numeric types (int/double/float), format them as numbers
108   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->default_value && desc->metadata.enum_values == NULL) {
109     // Check if this is a numeric callback by looking for numeric_range metadata
110     // Numeric callbacks have min/max range constraints set (max != 0 indicates a range was set)
111     if (desc->metadata.numeric_range.max != 0 || desc->metadata.numeric_range.min != 0) {
112       // This is a numeric callback option - extract and format the value
113       // Determine if it's int or double based on range (int max is 2147483647)
114       double default_double = 0.0;
115       if (desc->metadata.numeric_range.max <= 2147483647.0 && desc->metadata.numeric_range.min >= -2147483648.0) {
116         // Integer-ranged value (like port 1-65535) - stored as int
117         int default_int = 0;
118         memcpy(&default_int, desc->default_value, sizeof(int));
119         default_double = (double)default_int;
120       } else {
121         // Double-ranged value - stored as double
122         memcpy(&default_double, desc->default_value, sizeof(double));
123       }
124 
125       // Format with appropriate precision (remove trailing zeros for integers)
126       char formatted[32];
127       safe_snprintf(formatted, sizeof(formatted), "%.1f", default_double);
128 
129       // Remove trailing zeros after decimal point
130       char *dot = strchr(formatted, '.');
131       if (dot) {
132         char *end = formatted + strlen(formatted) - 1;
133         while (end > dot && *end == '0') {
134           end--;
135         }
136         if (*end == '.') {
137           *end = '\0';
138         } else {
139           *(end + 1) = '\0';
140         }
141       }
142 
143       return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", formatted);
144     }
145   }
146 
147   // For callback options with string defaults (no enum, no numeric range)
148   // Treat default_value as a const char* string pointer
149   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->default_value && desc->metadata.enum_values == NULL &&
150       desc->metadata.numeric_range.max == 0 && desc->metadata.numeric_range.min == 0) {
151     const char *str_default = (const char *)desc->default_value;
152     if (str_default && str_default[0] != '\0') {
153       return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", str_default);
154     }
155   }
156 
157   return options_format_default_value(desc->type, desc->default_value, buf, bufsize);
158 }

const
  char * get_option_help_placeholder_str (const option_descriptor_t *
  desc)


Get help placeholder string for an option type.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


Returns


Pointer to string literal ("INTEGER",
  "NUMBER", "STRING", "VALUE",
  "[BOOLEAN]"), custom placeholder, or empty string


Definition at line 37 of file builder.c.


37                                                                              {
38   if (!desc) {
39     return "";
40   }
41   // Check for custom placeholder first
42   if (desc->arg_placeholder != NULL) {
43     return desc->arg_placeholder;
44   }
45 
46   // For callback options, check if it's a boolean based on enum values
47   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->metadata.enum_values) {
48     // Check if enum values contain "true" and "false" (boolean-like)
49     const char *const *values = desc->metadata.enum_values;
50     bool has_true = false, has_false = false;
51 
52     for (int i = 0; values[i] != NULL; i++) {
53       if (strcmp(values[i], "true") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "yes") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "on") == 0) {
54         has_true = true;
55       }
56       if (strcmp(values[i], "false") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "no") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "off") == 0) {
57         has_false = true;
58       }
59     }
60 
61     // If it has both true-like and false-like values, it's boolean
62     if (has_true && has_false) {
63       return "BOOLEAN";
64     }
65   }
66 
67   return options_get_type_placeholder(desc->type);
68 }

bool option_applies_to_mode (const option_descriptor_t *
  desc, asciichat_mode_t mode, bool for_binary_help)


Check if an option applies to a specific mode using bitmask.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


mode Mode to check (use invalid value like -1 for binary help)


for_binary_help If true, show binary options; if false, hide them


Returns


true if option should be shown for this mode


Definition at line 171 of file builder.c.


171                                                                                                           {
172   if (!desc) {
173     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Descriptor is NULL");
174     return false;
175   }
176 
177   // When for_binary_help is true (i.e., for 'ascii-chat --help'),
178   // show options that apply to the default mode (DISCOVERY) or binary-level options only.
179   // Don't show mode-specific options for other modes (server, client, mirror, discovery-service).
180   if (for_binary_help) {
181     // Binary-level options or options that apply to discovery mode (the default)
182     option_mode_bitmask_t default_modes = OPTION_MODE_BINARY | OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY;
183     return (desc->mode_bitmask & default_modes) != 0 && !desc->hide_from_binary_help;
184   }
185 
186   // For mode-specific help (non-discovery modes), show only options for that mode.
187   // Do not show binary options here unless it also specifically applies to the mode.
188   // The 'mode' parameter is the actual mode (e.g., MODE_CLIENT, MODE_SERVER).
189   if (mode < 0 || mode > MODE_DISCOVERY) { // Use MODE_INVALID as upper bound for valid modes
190     return false;
191   }
192   option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bit = (1 << mode);
193 
194   // Check if it's a binary option. If so, only show if it also explicitly applies to this mode.
195   if ((desc->mode_bitmask & OPTION_MODE_BINARY) && !(desc->mode_bitmask & mode_bit)) {
196     return false; // Binary options not shown in mode-specific help unless also mode-specific
197   }
198 
199   bool applies = (desc->mode_bitmask & mode_bit) != 0;
200 
201   return applies && !desc->hide_from_mode_help;
202 }

  env_var_name, bool optional_arg)


Add callback option with optional argument support.


Parameters


builder Builder to add to


long_name Long option name


short_name Short option char (or '\0')


offset offsetof(struct, field)


default_value Pointer to default value (or NULL)


value_size sizeof(field_type)


parse_fn Custom parser function


help_text Help description


group Group name for help


required If true, option must be provided


env_var_name Environment variable fallback (or NULL)


optional_arg If true, argument is optional (parser receives NULL if not
  provided)

505

521     config->positional_args = NULL;
522     config->num_positional_args = 0;
523   }
524 
525   // Allocate and copy usage lines
526   if (builder->num_usage_lines > 0) {

528     if (!config->usage_lines) {
529       SAFE_FREE(config->descriptors);
530       SAFE_FREE(config->dependencies);

538   } else {
539     config->usage_lines = NULL;
540     config->num_usage_lines = 0;
541   }
542 
543   // Allocate and copy examples
544   if (builder->num_examples > 0) {
545     config->examples = SAFE_MALLOC(builder->num_examples * sizeof(example_descriptor_t), example_descriptor_t *);
546     if (!config->examples) {
547       SAFE_FREE(config->descriptors);
548       SAFE_FREE(config->dependencies);
549       SAFE_FREE(config->positional_args);

556     config->num_examples = builder->num_examples;
557   } else {
558     config->examples = NULL;
559     config->num_examples = 0;
560   }
561 
562   // Allocate and copy modes
563   if (builder->num_modes > 0) {
564     config->modes = SAFE_MALLOC(builder->num_modes * sizeof(help_mode_descriptor_t), help_mode_descriptor_t *);
565     if (!config->modes) {
566       SAFE_FREE(config->descriptors);
567       SAFE_FREE(config->dependencies);
568       SAFE_FREE(config->positional_args);
569       SAFE_FREE(config->usage_lines);
570       SAFE_FREE(config->examples);

572       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate modes");

574     }
575     memcpy(config->modes, builder->modes, builder->num_modes * sizeof(help_mode_descriptor_t));

577   } else {
578     config->modes = NULL;
579     config->num_modes = 0;
580   }
581 
582   // Allocate and copy custom sections
583   if (builder->num_custom_sections > 0) {
584     config->custom_sections =
585         SAFE_MALLOC(builder->num_custom_sections * sizeof(custom_section_descriptor_t), custom_section_descriptor_t *);
586     if (!config->custom_sections) {
587       SAFE_FREE(config->descriptors);
588       SAFE_FREE(config->dependencies);

600   } else {
601     config->custom_sections = NULL;
602     config->num_custom_sections = 0;
603   }
604 
605   config->struct_size = builder->struct_size;
606   config->program_name = builder->program_name;
607   config->description = builder->description;
608 
609   // Initialize memory management
610   config->owned_strings = builder->owned_strings_builder;
611   config->num_owned_strings = builder->num_owned_strings_builder;
612   config->owned_strings_capacity = builder->owned_strings_builder_capacity;
613 
614   // Clear builder's ownership so it doesn't free them
615   builder->owned_strings_builder = NULL;
616   builder->num_owned_strings_builder = 0;
617   builder->owned_strings_builder_capacity = 0;
618

1340   // No positional args defined - but we got some positional arguments

1364     char **remaining = (arg_index + 1 < remaining_argc) ? &remaining_argv[arg_index + 1] : NULL;
1365     int num_remaining = remaining_argc - arg_index - 1;
1366     char *error_msg = NULL;
1367 
1368     // Call custom parser
1369     int consumed = pos_arg->parse_fn(arg, options_struct, remaining, num_remaining, &error_msg);

1371     if (consumed < 0) {
1372       // Parse error
1373       if (error_msg) {
1374         log_error("Error parsing positional argument '%s': %s", pos_arg->name, error_msg);
1375         free(error_msg);
1376       } else {
1377         log_error("Error parsing positional argument '%s': %s", pos_arg->name, arg);
1378       }
1379       return ERROR_USAGE;
1380     }
1381 
1382     // Advance by consumed args (usually 1, but can be more for multi-arg parsers)

1384   }
1385 
1386   // Check for extra unconsumed positional arguments

1388     log_error("Error: Unexpected positional argument '%s'", remaining_argv[arg_index]);

1390   }
1391 
1392   return ASCIICHAT_OK;

asciichat_error_t
  options_config_set_defaults (const options_config_t * config, void *
  options_struct)


Definition at line 1399 of file builder.c.


1399                                                                                                     {
1400   if (!config || !options_struct) {
1401     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or options struct is NULL");
1402   }
1403 
1404   char *base = (char *)options_struct;
1405 
1406   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1407     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1408     void *field = base + desc->offset;
1409 
1410     // Check environment variable first (primary)
1411     const char *env_value = NULL;
1412     if (desc->env_var_name) {
1413       env_value = SAFE_GETENV(desc->env_var_name);
1414     }
1415     // Check shorter alias names as fallback if primary not set
1416     if (!env_value) {
1417       if (desc->env_var_name && strcmp(desc->env_var_name, "ASCII_CHAT_PORT") == 0) {
1418         env_value = SAFE_GETENV("PORT");
1419       } else if (desc->env_var_name && strcmp(desc->env_var_name, "ASCII_CHAT_WEBSOCKET_PORT") == 0) {
1420         env_value = SAFE_GETENV("WS_PORT");
1421       }
1422     }
1423 
1424     // Use handler registry to apply environment variables and defaults
1425     if (desc->type >= 0 && desc->type < (int)(NUM_OPTION_TYPES)) {
1426       if (g_builder_handlers[desc->type].apply_env) {
1427         g_builder_handlers[desc->type].apply_env(field, env_value, desc);
1428       }
1429     } else if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->parse_fn && desc->default_value) {
1430       // Special handling for callbacks with parse_fn
1431       char *error_msg = NULL;
1432       desc->parse_fn(NULL, field, &error_msg);
1433       if (error_msg) {
1434         free(error_msg);
1435       }
1436     }
1437   }
1438 
1439   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1440 }

asciichat_error_t
  options_config_validate (const options_config_t * config, const void *
  options_struct, char ** error_message)


Definition at line 1838 of file builder.c.


1839                                                                 {
1840   if (!config || !options_struct) {
1841     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or options struct is NULL");
1842   }
1843 
1844   const char *base = (const char *)options_struct;
1845 
1846   // Check required fields
1847   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1848     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1849     if (!desc->required)
1850       continue;
1851 
1852     const void *field = base + desc->offset;
1853     // Use handler registry to check if option is set (for required field validation)
1854     bool is_set = true;
1855     if (desc->type >= 0 && desc->type < (int)(NUM_OPTION_TYPES)) {
1856       if (g_builder_handlers[desc->type].is_set) {
1857         is_set = g_builder_handlers[desc->type].is_set(field, desc);
1858       }
1859     }
1860 
1861     if (!is_set) {
1862       if (error_message) {
1863         if (desc->env_var_name) {
1864           asprintf(error_message, "Required option --%s is not set (set %s env var or use --%s)", desc->long_name,
1865                    desc->env_var_name, desc->long_name);
1866         } else {
1867           asprintf(error_message, "Required option --%s is not set", desc->long_name);
1868         }
1869       }
1870       return ERROR_USAGE;
1871     }
1872   }
1873 
1874   // Check dependencies
1875   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_dependencies; i++) {
1876     const option_dependency_t *dep = &config->dependencies[i];
1877 
1878     bool option_is_set = is_option_set(config, options_struct, dep->option_name);
1879     bool depends_is_set = is_option_set(config, options_struct, dep->depends_on);
1880 
1881     switch (dep->type) {
1882     case DEPENDENCY_REQUIRES:
1883       if (option_is_set && !depends_is_set) {
1884         if (error_message) {
1885           if (dep->error_message) {
1886             *error_message = platform_strdup(dep->error_message);
1887           } else {
1888             asprintf(error_message, "Option --%s requires --%s to be set", dep->option_name, dep->depends_on);
1889           }
1890         }
1891         return ERROR_USAGE;
1892       }
1893       break;
1894 
1895     case DEPENDENCY_CONFLICTS:
1896       if (option_is_set && depends_is_set) {
1897         if (error_message) {
1898           if (dep->error_message) {
1899             *error_message = platform_strdup(dep->error_message);
1900           } else {
1901             asprintf(error_message, "Option --%s conflicts with --%s", dep->option_name, dep->depends_on);
1902           }
1903         }
1904         return ERROR_USAGE;
1905       }
1906       break;
1907 
1908     case DEPENDENCY_IMPLIES:
1909       // Implies is handled during parsing, not validation
1910       break;
1911     }
1912   }
1913 
1914   // Run custom validators
1915   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1916     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1917     if (!desc->validate)
1918       continue;
1919 
1920     char *custom_error = NULL;
1921     if (!desc->validate(options_struct, &custom_error)) {
1922       if (error_message) {
1923         *error_message = custom_error;
1924       } else {
1925         free(custom_error);
1926       }
1927       return ERROR_USAGE;
1928     }
1929   }
1930 
1931   // Cross-field validation: Check for conflicting color options
1932   // Cannot use --color with --color-mode none
1933   const options_t *opts = (const options_t *)options_struct;
1934   if (opts->color && opts->color_mode == TERM_COLOR_NONE) {
1935     if (error_message) {
1936       asprintf(error_message, "Option --color cannot be used with --color-mode=none (conflicting color settings)");
1937     }
1938     return ERROR_USAGE;
1939   }
1940 
1941   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1942 }
